nativeRange.habitat: Lagoon and seaward coral reefs, typically at depths of 5–40 m

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting a varied diet including frozen mysis shrimp, frozen brine shrimp, herbivore preparations, and high-quality flaked or pellet foods. Feeding meaty and algae-based foods alternately tends to keep specimens in good condition.

avoidWith: similarly sized fish in confined spaces

summary: A small, hardy reef damselfish displaying striking purple-to-yellow coloration with warm pink and orange accents. Generally considered suitable for beginners and reef-safe; tends to be territorial toward conspecifics and similarly sized fish.
careNotes: Tracey's Damselfish is a small, hardy species (generally reaching around 4–4.5 cm) generally well-suited to beginning reef keepers. It tends to ignore corals and invertebrates, making it widely considered reef-safe. While it will typically leave other fish alone, it has been known to be territorial toward conspecifics or similarly sized fish — particularly in smaller quarters. Providing ample live rock with crevices and caves tends to reduce territorial aggression by giving each fish its own territory. Keeping groups of three or more odd-numbered fish in suitably sized systems has been reported to distribute aggression and produce a more natural display. FishBase records this species across a broad Western Pacific range — from the Philippines and Mariana Islands through the Caroline and Marshall Islands to Tonga and Indonesia — though it is often imported from Fiji in the aquarium trade. Lifespan in captivity has been reported at around 5–7 years under good conditions. One source (saltwaterfish.com) listed adult size as 3 inches (7.6 cm), which appears to be a data error; FishBase, LiveAquaria, and fishy.wiki consistently place the maximum standard length at 4.3–4.5 cm. Temperature: LiveAquaria recommends 72–78°F (22–26°C) while FishBase wild-data shows 27.6–29°C; an averaged range of approximately 24–28°C is used here as the best husbandry estimate.
breedingNotes: Males have been reported to guard and aerate adhesive egg clutches deposited on substrate surfaces (reef rock or rubble). Breeding in captivity is documented to occur but is not commonly pursued in the hobby. Pairs have been described as forming distinct breeding partnerships.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (4.5 cm SL confirmed), native range (Western Pacific: Philippines, Marshall Islands, Caroline Islands, Mariana Islands, Tonga), and temperature (wild data 27.6–29°C — warmer than LiveAquaria recommendation; final value averaged across sources).

additional.name: fishy.wiki
additional.notes: Confirmed adult size 4.3 cm, tank minimum ~113 L, pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 1.020–1.025, semi-aggressive temperament, reef-safe, omnivore diet. Consistent with primary source.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com
additional.notes: Cross-reference; listed adult size as 3 inches (7.6 cm) — likely a data error, as three other sources consistently report 4.3–4.5 cm. Lifespan 5–7 years used from this source. Temperature range 74–82°F (23–28°C) factored into averaged final range.

fish.breedingNotes: Males guard and aerate adhesive egg clutches on substrate. Breeding has been reported in captivity but is uncommon. Distinct pair formation observed during breeding season.
